# Memories — product requirements

## Document control

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Author** | TBD |
| **PM / owner** | TBD |
| **Reviewers** | Engineering, design (TBD) |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Version** | 0.1 |
| **Last updated** | 2026-04-22 |
| **Template used** | Structure aligned with `.cursor/skills/product-manager/reference.md`; document-control table style aligned with `docs/templates/product-requirements-template.md` |
| **Related docs** | [README.md](../README.md); [docs/tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md); future [docs/technical-design.md](technical-design.md), [docs/development-plan.md](development-plan.md) |
| **External references** | Dashboard / backend prototype context: [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73) (Notion). **Note:** this page is delivered as a client-rendered Notion app; requirements here were drafted from product intent and this repo’s stated scope until the handoff is exported or pasted for literal traceability. |

---

## Executive summary

- **Recommendation:** Proceed with a phased **Memories** capability that lets users **capture**, **organize**, and **revisit** personal or family memories using **images**, **audio**, and **text** (including **transcripts** derived from audio where enabled).
- **What:** A **Memories** product (this monorepo: web + API + shared contracts) that persists and surfaces memory entries with rich media, with a path to embed or deep-link from a broader **Dashboard** in separate repositories.
- **Why now:** Establishes a dedicated surface for multimodal memory capture before Dashboard composition matures; reduces scatter across camera rolls, voice memos, and notes apps.
- **Success (headline):** Users successfully **create** memories with at least one media type, **find** them again, and **trust** retention and privacy boundaries; engineering can trace behavior to stable **FR** / **NFR** IDs for TDD and QA.
- **Top risks:** Auth and identity alignment with the future **Dashboard**; media **cost** and **latency** (uploads, transcription); **privacy** expectations for sensitive family content; scope creep into full Dashboard features inside Memories.

---

## Background & context

- **Company/product context:** **Memories** is one product in a broader **Ohana** direction: a memory app for **images**, **audio**, and **text/transcripts**, eventually surfaced as part of a **complete Dashboard** alongside other modules. The Dashboard and other services **live in other git repositories**; this repo owns the **Memories** experience and API boundary as described in [README.md](../README.md).
- **Prior work:** Monorepo scaffold exists (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, `packages/shared`) with stack choices captured in [docs/tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md). Persistence, object storage, transcription, and Dashboard auth integration are **open** decisions to lock in PRD/TDD revisions.
- **Evidence:** TBD (user research, support signals, competitive notes). Until captured, treat goals as **hypothesis** and validate after first internal dogfood.

---

## Problem statement

- **Current experience:** People store meaningful moments across **photos**, **voice memos**, and **text**, often **unlinked** and **hard to search** by story, date, or participant. Family memories are especially fragmented across devices and messengers.
- **Pain points:** **Friction** capturing voice + photo in one place; **no transcript** or poor transcript quality; **weak retrieval**; uncertainty about **who can see** shared memories.
- **Why existing solutions fail:** General galleries lack **narrative** and **transcript**; notes apps lack **audio-first** capture; messaging threads lose structure and are not owned as a personal archive.

---

## Goals & non-goals

### Goals

1. Users can **create** a memory that includes **one or more** of: image(s), audio recording or upload, and text (title/body).
2. Users can **view** a memory with clear presentation of each media type and text (including transcript when present).
3. Users can **list** or **browse** their memories with enough **metadata** (e.g. time, title) to support retrieval (exact IA TBD with design).
4. **Privacy and access** rules are explicit for v1 (even if v1 is single-user) so Dashboard **multi-tenant** evolution does not require a rewrite of core objects.
5. **Operational** clarity: failures (upload, transcription) are visible and recoverable per **FR** below.

### Non-goals (current draft)

- Building the full **Dashboard** shell, navigation, or unrelated modules inside this repo.
- **Native** mobile apps (browser-first unless reprioritized; see [README.md](../README.md)).
- **Social network**-style discovery, public feeds, or algorithmic recommendations.
- **Guaranteed** real-time collaborative editing of a single memory across users in **MVP** (may be phased; real-time patterns in this repo’s skills are available when scope demands).

---

## Target users & segments

| Segment | Description | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Primary memory keeper** | Adult who captures and curates family or personal memories | Primary |
| **Contributor** | Trusted family member who adds audio/photos to shared memories | Secondary (post-MVP unless Dashboard identity lands early) |
| **Viewer** | Someone with read-only access to a subset of memories | Secondary / phased |

### Personas (draft)

- **Primary memory keeper:** Wants low-friction capture on phone/desktop, readable transcripts, and confidence that media is **not** exposed outside the household/account boundary they expect.

---

## Solution overview

- **Concept:** **Memories** is a structured **archive** of moments. Each **memory** is a first-class object combining optional **image assets**, **audio**, **user-authored text**, and optionally **machine-generated transcript text** linked to the audio.
- **Key capabilities:** Create/read/update/delete memories (scope per phase); attach and display media; show transcription **status** and text when available; align with Dashboard entry points when integrated.
- **Key flows (high level):** (1) Quick capture → saved draft or memory. (2) Review memory → play audio, read transcript, see images. (3) Recover from failed upload or transcription. (4) Future: open memory from **Dashboard** with consistent identity.

---

## Scope & phasing

### In scope (MVP / v1 — draft)

- CRUD for **memory** entities with **at least one** content type present before **publish** (exact “draft vs published” model TBD).
- **Image** attachment: upload, store, retrieve for display (limits TBD in TDD: size, count, formats).
- **Audio** attachment: record or upload (which of record vs upload is MVP — **open**; pick one for v1 to ship).
- **Text:** title and/or body fields with documented length limits (Unicode code points; align with existing repo patterns when implemented).
- **Transcription:** async pipeline with user-visible **pending / ready / failed** states (provider-agnostic requirement; vendor in TDD).
- Basic **list + detail** UX in `apps/web` backed by `apps/api` contracts in `packages/shared`.

### Out of scope (near term)

- Full Dashboard **layout**, unrelated widgets, or cross-product analytics **unless** explicitly pulled in by a Dashboard initiative doc.
- **Offline-first** sync (online-first unless PRD amended).

### Future / later phases

- **Dashboard** integration: SSO/session from platform IdP, deep links, optional embedded Memories views ([docs/tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md) open items).
- **Sharing** within family groups with roles (viewer/contributor/owner).
- **Search** across transcript and text; optional **semantic** search (product decision).

### Dependencies

- **Product / platform:** Dashboard identity, navigation contract, and whether Memories is **standalone**, **embedded**, or **both**.
- **Org / legal / compliance:** Media involving minors; retention; export/deletion policy (TDD + legal review).
- **Data / integrations:** Object storage, transcription vendor, email or magic-link auth if reused from sister patterns.

---

## User journeys & key scenarios

### Scenario 1: Capture a memory with photo and voice

- **Actor:** Primary memory keeper.
- **Trigger:** User finishes a moment they want to keep.
- **Happy path:** User creates a memory, adds a photo, records short audio, saves; sees memory in list; opens detail and confirms audio plays and image renders.
- **Edge cases / failure modes:** Large image rejected with clear error; audio permission denied; partial save (TBD: transactional rules in TDD).

### Scenario 2: Transcription completes later

- **Actor:** Primary memory keeper.
- **Trigger:** User saves memory with audio eligible for transcription.
- **Happy path:** UI shows **pending**, then transcript appears without user losing other fields.
- **Edge cases / failure modes:** Transcription fails; user sees **failed** with retry or manual edit of text (exact recovery UX TBD).

### Scenario 3: Open from Dashboard (future)

- **Actor:** Authenticated Dashboard user.
- **Trigger:** User selects Memories from Dashboard.
- **Happy path:** User lands in Memories with same identity and sees their library (details depend on Dashboard handoff — reconcile with Notion doc when available).

---

## Functional requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Source | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **FR-001** | User can **create** a memory. | P0 | Goals | |
| **FR-002** | User can **view** a memory’s details (text + each attached media type that exists). | P0 | Goals | |
| **FR-003** | User can **update** editable fields on a memory they own (exact fields TBD). | P0 | Journeys | Versioning/conflicts TBD in TDD. |
| **FR-004** | User can **delete** a memory they own with a **confirming** step. | P1 | Safety | Irreversibility messaging. |
| **FR-005** | User can **attach** at least one **image** to a memory and see it on detail view. | P0 | MVP scope | Multiple images P1 unless design says otherwise. |
| **FR-006** | User can **attach** **audio** to a memory and **play** it on detail view. | P0 | MVP scope | Record vs upload split TBD. |
| **FR-007** | System stores **user-authored text** (title and/or body) on a memory with **documented** length limits. | P0 | MVP scope | Align Zod + UI counters in implementation. |
| **FR-008** | For memories with eligible audio, system attempts **transcription** and persists transcript text when successful. | P1 | MVP scope | Can be P0 if product bets on transcript-first; currently P1 to allow phased vendor work. |
| **FR-009** | User-visible **transcription status**: at minimum **pending**, **ready**, **failed** (labels can match UI copy). | P1 | FR-008 | |
| **FR-010** | User can **list** memories in reverse-chronological or other defined default sort (pick one in design). | P0 | Goals | |
| **FR-011** | System rejects **oversize** or **unsupported** media with a **clear**, **actionable** message. | P0 | NFR quality | Thresholds in TDD. |
| **FR-012** | Authenticated user only sees memories **authorized** to them (v1 may be “all memories for the signed-in user”). | P0 | Privacy | Sharing rules later. |

*Priority: P0 = launch blocker for first meaningful release; P1 = important immediately after or same release if staffing allows.*

---

## User stories & acceptance criteria

### Epic: Memory CRUD

**Story:** As a **memory keeper**, I want to **create and open memories**, so that I can **preserve and revisit moments**.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given a signed-in user, when they create a memory with required fields per policy, then it appears in the memory list and opens on detail without error.
- Given a signed-in user, when they open a memory they own, then they see text fields and each attached media type that exists.

### Epic: Media capture

**Story:** As a **memory keeper**, I want to **add photos and audio** to a memory, so that the moment is **multimodal** and faithful.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given a memory draft or edit flow, when the user attaches an allowed image, then the detail view renders that image after save.
- Given a memory draft or edit flow, when the user attaches allowed audio, then playback works on supported browsers listed in TDD.

### Epic: Transcription

**Story:** As a **memory keeper**, I want **transcripts of my audio**, so that I can **read and search** what was said.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given audio eligible for transcription, when processing completes successfully, then transcript text is shown alongside audio.
- Given processing failure, then the user sees **failed** state and guidance per FR-009 (exact copy TBD).

---

## Non-functional requirements

| ID | Category | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **NFR-001** | Security | All client–server traffic uses **HTTPS** in deployed environments. | P0 | Baseline |
| **NFR-002** | Privacy | Media and transcripts are **access-controlled** consistently with account identity; no public URLs without explicit sharing feature. | P0 | Signed URLs pattern likely in TDD |
| **NFR-003** | Reliability | Core API exposes **health** and structured errors for client handling. | P1 | Exists today at `/health`; extend as features land |
| **NFR-004** | Accessibility | New UI meets **WCAG 2.2 AA** intent for primary flows (capture, list, detail). | P1 | Align `developer-frontend-ui` skill |
| **NFR-005** | Performance | Define targets in TDD (e.g. p95 list load, upload chunking if used); MVP defaults to **good faith** online use. | P2 | Quantify with metrics |
| **NFR-006** | Observability | Server logs include **request correlation** suitable for debugging upload/transcription failures. | P1 | Align backend skill + pino patterns |

---

## Analytics & experimentation

- **North-star (proposal):** Weekly **active** memory creators and **memories with multimodal** content (image + audio).
- **Supporting metrics:** Transcription **success rate**, upload **failure rate**, time-to-first-memory for new accounts.
- **Events / properties:** TBD when analytics stack chosen (may be shared with Dashboard).
- **Experimentation:** TBD.

---

## Go-to-market & rollout

- **Launch definition:** Internal dogfood cohort can create/list/view memories with image + audio; transcription either working behind flag or explicitly deferred with FR-008/009 scoped.
- **Audience / comms:** TBD.
- **Support / ops:** Playbooks for media failures and account deletion (ties to NFR-002).
- **Migration:** N/A for greenfield; forward migrations covered in TDD when persistence lands.

---

## Timeline & milestones

| Milestone | Description | Target |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **PRD v0.2** | Reconcile with exported Dashboard / backend handoff; firm MVP priorities | TBD |
| **TDD v0.1** | Storage, auth boundary, media + transcription design | TBD |
| **MVP candidate** | P0 FRs satisfied in staging | TBD |

---

## Risks & mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood / impact | Mitigation | Owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Dashboard auth model changes Memories assumptions | High / High | Early **interface** agreement; keep auth abstraction in TDD | TBD |
| Transcription cost or latency unacceptable | Med / Med | Budget caps, async UX, provider abstraction | TBD |
| Scope creep into Dashboard | Med / Med | Strict **non-goals**; link out to Dashboard PRD | PM |

---

## Open questions

| # | Question | Owner | Due / status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | What from [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73) becomes **hard** requirements vs suggestions? | PM + Eng | **Blocked** until doc exported or pasted |
| 2 | MVP: **audio record in browser**, **upload only**, or both? | PM + Design | Open |
| 3 | Is v1 **single-user only**, or is shared family access in first release? | PM | Open |
| 4 | **Draft** vs **published** memories — required for MVP? | PM + Design | Open |
| 5 | Dashboard: **deep link** vs **iframe/embed** vs standalone — which is first integration? | Platform | Open |

---

## Appendix

### Glossary

- **Memory:** A user-owned record combining optional image(s), audio, text, and optional transcript.
- **Dashboard:** Separate product/repository that will orchestrate broader navigation; Memories integrates over agreed HTTP/auth contracts.

### References

- [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73) — platform/backend context (reconcile when exportable).
- This repo: [README.md](../README.md), [docs/tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md).

---

## Handoff (engineering alignment)

- **Technical design:** Produce `docs/technical-design.md` using `.cursor/skills/technical-designer/` — map components, APIs, storage, and auth to **FR-*** / **NFR-*** IDs; link back to this PRD without restating it in full.
- **Execution plan:** When scope is draft-approved, run `.cursor/skills/development-planner/` with this PRD plus wireframes/TDD inputs.
- **Coordination:** Per `.cursor/skills/developer-manager/`, keep **single sources of truth** (this PRD + TDD + tickets), sequence work with explicit owners, and plan tests/observability in the same slices as features.

# Memories — product requirements

## Document control

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Author** | TBD |
| **PM / owner** | TBD (handoff lists Ben Cerezo as product owner for Ohana Way prototype) |
| **Reviewers** | Engineering, design, compliance (TBD) |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Version** | 0.2 |
| **Last updated** | 2026-04-22 |
| **Template used** | Structure aligned with `.cursor/skills/product-manager/reference.md`; document-control table style aligned with `docs/templates/product-requirements-template.md` |
| **Related docs** | [README.md](../README.md); [docs/tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md); [docs/Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md) (**platform / prototype backend source**); future [docs/technical-design.md](technical-design.md), [docs/development-plan.md](development-plan.md) |
| **External references** | Notion mirror (client-rendered): [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73). **Authoritative copy for engineering:** exported markdown in this repo (`docs/Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md`). |

---

## Executive summary

- **Recommendation:** Proceed with **Memories** as the multimodal **memory archive** subsystem (images, audio, text, transcripts), implemented in this monorepo and **aligned** with the **Ohana Way** prototype backend handoff for **data shape**, **capture resilience**, **HIPAA-oriented handling** of memory PHI, and future **Dashboard / platform** integration.
- **What:** APIs and web UX for **memory lifecycle** (create, list, detail, media upload, async transcription, tags/reactions as phased), designed so the broader product (Guides, Practices, Clients, Family) can attach without rewriting core memory objects.
- **Why now:** The handoff defines a **closed pilot** with production expectations for **Memory Capture** and related flows; this repo should not diverge casually from those constraints even when features ship incrementally.
- **Success (headline):** Guides or end users (depending on integration mode) can **capture** and **retrieve** memories without **losing work** to connectivity issues where the client owns the flow; transcripts and media meet **access, audit, and logging** rules appropriate to PHI; requirements remain **traceable** to stable IDs for TDD and QA.
- **Top risks:** **HIPAA / BAA** long-lead on vendors touching audio and transcripts; **offline / retry** capture complexity; **tenant and RBAC** split across repos causing gaps; **scope creep** (implementing full Ohana Way inside Memories instead of integrating).

---

## Background & context

- **Company/product context:** **Ohana Way** (see handoff) is a **Guide-facing PWA** for licensed professionals supporting elders and families; its signature feature is **Memory Capture** during home visits. **Memories** (this repo) is the **memory-focused** service the README describes—photos, voice, transcription—eventually part of a **complete Dashboard** and the wider platform. Other surfaces (full G1 dashboard, G6 inbox, practice admin) may live in **other repositories**; this PRD still treats the handoff as the **canonical product and compliance context** for anything labeled a **Memory**.
- **Prior work:** Monorepo scaffold exists (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, `packages/shared`). [docs/Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md) adds the **canonical data model** (Practice → Client → Memory → MemoryMedia / MemoryTranscript / tags / reactions / comments), **Memory Capture** four-step flow, **save pipeline** with offline tolerance, **AI prompt suggestion** and **tag suggestion** patterns, **auth/RBAC** direction, **HIPAA** expectations, retention, notifications, and **MVP pilot** scope for the **whole** Ohana Way—not all of which this repo implements in v1.
- **Evidence:** Pilot scale and quality bar are stated in the handoff (2–5 Guides, 10–25 clients, production-ready correctness for capture). User research pointers in handoff remain **TBD** for this doc’s metrics section until linked.

### Platform alignment (Memories repo vs. Ohana Way handoff)

If another service owns tenants, this repo consumes **identity context** from it—**decision in TDD**. |
| **Client ≠ User** | Client is subject of care; access via `ClientAccess` roles ([Handoff §4.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)) | Memory reads/writes must be compatible with **ClientAccess**-style scoping when integrated; avoid “only user_id” shortcuts that block Family/Guide rules. |

---

## Problem statement

- **Current experience:** Same as v0.1: meaningful moments scattered across photos, voice memos, and text. For **Ohana Way**, add: during visits, **unreliable connectivity** risks **losing** irreplaceable client stories if capture is not offline-tolerant.
- **Pain points:** Friction linking **photo + audio + transcript**; weak retrieval; **trust** (who sees PHI); **compliance** burden if memory data is treated casually.
- **Why existing solutions fail:** Handoff §1.1: generic tools do not match **facilitated**, **family-archive** semantics or practice-scoped compliance.

---

## Goals & non-goals

### Goals

1. Users (or Guides on behalf of clients) can **create** memories with **one or more** of: images, audio, user text; system can attach **machine transcript** to eligible audio.
2. Users can **view** and **list** memories with clear media presentation and **transcription state** (pending / ready / failed).
3. **Data model alignment:** Memory supports **multiple** `MemoryMedia` rows and a **separable** transcript record consistent with handoff §4.2 (not a single fixed photo/audio pair only).
4. **Resilience:** Where this app owns capture/save, **work is not lost** to transient connectivity ([Handoff §2.5, §5.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md))—local queue + retry through **TDD-defined** mechanism.
5. **Compliance-oriented behavior:** No PHI content in application/analytics logs; audit expectations for PHI access **when this service participates in HIPAA scope** ([Handoff §8.3, §11.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
6. **AI-assisted capture (when built):** Prompt suggestion with **<2s** target and **offline-safe fallback** copy if AI unavailable ([Handoff §6.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).

### Non-goals (current draft)

- Owning the entire **Ohana Way** pilot in this repo: **G1** dashboard, **G6** unified inbox, **G4** AI Guide **UI** (unless explicitly merged here), practice billing, full Guide onboarding—unless product re-scopes.
- **Native** installed apps (PWA/browser per [README](../README.md) and handoff §3.1).
- **SMS** notifications (handoff §10.1: future).
- Implementing **full** Postgres RLS in isolation **without** platform identity story—defer to TDD that names the auth source of truth.

---

## Target users & segments

| Segment | Description | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Guide (Ohana Way)** | Professional capturing memories **for** a client during visits | Primary **when** this repo backs Guide flows |
| **Primary memory keeper / family** | Personal or family archive use (Dashboard / consumer framing) | Primary **when** product mode is direct-to-family |
| **Client** | Elder subject of care; often not a logged-in user in v1 ([Handoff §1.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)) | Subject; access rules via ClientAccess |
| **Family member** | Read-only or limited write (comments/reactions) per phase ([Handoff §1.2, §7.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)) | Secondary until family UI ships |

*Priority depends on which initiative ships first; engineering should not break **ClientAccess**-shaped authorization.*

---

## Solution overview

- **Concept:** A **Memory** aggregates user- or Guide-authored metadata (name, room, optional era/valence per handoff §4.3), **media** in object storage, **transcript** rows, optional **tags**, and (phase-dependent) **reactions/comments** for C4-style detail.
- **Key capabilities:** CRUD/list/detail; signed upload pipeline; async transcription; optional AI prompt and tag suggestions with guardrails; pagination for memory grids (cursor, ~20/page per handoff §5.5).
- **Key flows:** (1) Capture flow (handoff §5.1). (2) Async transcription + optional tag pass (§5.1, §6.3). (3) Memory detail with playback and transcript. (4) Platform integration: same identity/tenant context as Dashboard.

---

## Scope & phasing

### In scope (Memories repo — near-term)

- Memory **CRUD** and **list/detail** with **practice/client scoping** once tenant model is chosen (or single-tenant stub with migration path—**TDD**).
- **MemoryMedia** model (multiple attachments); **MemoryTranscript** separate from media ([Handoff §4.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Upload** via signed URLs to object storage; **server** creates canonical `memory_id` after uploads ([Handoff §5.1 save pipeline](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Async transcription** job with states; optional **Web Push** “transcribed” event is **platform** concern—Memories may emit domain events consumed elsewhere ([Handoff §5.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Suggest prompt** endpoint behavior and **fallback** question when AI fails ([Handoff §6.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Tag suggestions** after transcript (human confirm) as **P1/P2** per staffing ([Handoff §6.3](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Reactions and comments** on memory detail aligned with C4 when pilot requires ([Handoff §12.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md))—may trail core capture.

### In scope (full Ohana pilot — cross-repo)

- Items in handoff **§12.1 Must-have** not listed above ship in **other** apps/services (auth+MFA, G1/G2/G3 shell, inbox, AI Guide, family invite read-only) with **contracts** this repo honors.

### Out of scope (near term)

- Telephony/video, rich analytics dashboard for practices ([Handoff §12.3](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **PDF memory book** export ([Handoff §12.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md))—fast-follow.

### Dependencies

- **BAA execution** for cloud, DB, object storage, STT, AI, email, push, Sentry, observability ([Handoff §8.5](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)) before production PHI.
- **STT vendor** selection ([Handoff §13.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)) drives audio format and pipeline.
- **Dashboard / identity** service for JWT/session claims (`practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id`, roles).

---

## User journeys & key scenarios

### Scenario 1: Guide — Memory Capture (happy path)

- **Actor:** Guide with `GUIDE_PRIMARY` or `GUIDE_SUPPORT` on client.
- **Trigger:** Opens Memory Capture from client’s Memories tab ([Handoff §5.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Happy path:** Photo → name + room → AI prompt → record audio → review → save; sees success; memory appears in client grid; transcript appears after async processing.
- **Edge cases:** AI prompt timeout → **generic fallback** question. Long audio → warn at 15 minutes. Offline during save → **queued retry** until success.

### Scenario 2: Transcription completes later

- Same as v0.1; add: optional **tag suggestions** appear after transcript; user accepts/rejects ([Handoff §6.3](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).

### Scenario 3: Family read-only consumer

- **Actor:** Family with appropriate `ClientAccess` role.
- **Trigger:** Opens link or bare-bones UI ([Handoff §12.1](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Happy path:** Views allowed memories per `sharing_visibility` (when implemented [Handoff §4.3](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)); cannot create memories unless matrix says so ([Handoff §7.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).

### Scenario 4: Open from Dashboard (future)

- Authenticated user lands with consistent identity; sees authorized memory library.

---

---

## User stories & acceptance criteria

### Epic: Memory CRUD and list

**Story:** As a **Guide**, I want to **create and browse memories for my client**, so that the family has an **archive** of meaningful objects.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given authenticated Guide with access to client, when they complete capture and save, then a new memory appears in the client’s paginated list with correct name/room and media placeholders resolved after upload.
- Given Family with read access, when they open a memory allowed by visibility, then they see media and transcript but **not** practice-only fields (e.g. GuideNote — out of memory scope).

### Epic: Resilient save

**Story:** As a **Guide**, I want **captures to survive bad Wi‑Fi**, so I **never lose** a client’s story ([Handoff §2.5](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given a failed upload or API error on save, when the device reconnects, then the client retries until success or explicit user cancel policy (TDD), without duplicate memory rows (**FR-013**).

### Epic: Transcription and tags

**Story:** As a **Guide**, I want a **transcript and suggested tags**, so I can **review and organize** faster.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Given audio saved, when transcription completes, then transcript row exists and UI shows ready state.
- Given transcript exists, when tag suggestion runs, then user can accept/reject suggestions before persisting tags (**FR-016**).

---

| **NFR-005** | Performance | Prompt suggestion **p95 < 2s** under nominal load; transcription **async** acceptable 30s–3min ([Handoff §5.1, §6.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)). | P1 | |
| **NFR-006** | Observability | **Request correlation**; log **metadata only** (ids, timings, status)—**no** transcript/audio/content in logs ([Handoff §11.1, §5.1 PHI](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)). | P0 | Redaction middleware |

| **NFR-010** | Operations | **Upload failure** and **AI error** rates monitored; alerts per handoff §11.3 thresholds where applicable. | P1 | Tune per environment |

---

## Analytics & experimentation

- **North-star (proposal):** Successful **captures per active client** and **transcription success rate** ([Handoff §11.2](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).
- **Supporting metrics:** Upload **retry rate**, AI prompt **latency**, memory list **p95** load.
- **PHI:** Analytics events must follow **generic** or **redacted** payload rules ([Handoff §10.3](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md))—product decision on detail level.
- **Experimentation:** TBD.

---

## Go-to-market & rollout

- **Launch definition (pilot-aligned):** Matches handoff **§12.1** subset owned by this service: resilient capture, storage, transcript, detail interactions, observability hooks.
- **Audience:** Closed pilot Guides and families per handoff §1.3.
- **Support / ops:** Breach runbook, backup/restore expectations ([Handoff §8.4, §11.4](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md))—platform ops.
- **Migration:** Forward-only schema migrations with soft-delete preference ([Handoff §4.4, §9.6](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md)).

# Memories — product requirements

## Document control

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Author** | Ken Levy |
| **PM / owner** | Ben Cerezo |
| **Reviewers** | Engineering, design, compliance (TBD) |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Version** | 0.3 |
| **Last updated** | 2026-04-22 |
| **Template used** | `.cursor/skills/product-manager/reference.md`; `docs/templates/product-requirements-template.md` (control table) |
| **Related docs** | [README.md](../README.md); [tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md); [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md); TBD: [technical-design.md](technical-design.md), [development-plan.md](development-plan.md) |
| **External references** | [Notion mirror](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73) — **authoritative:** repo handoff markdown above |

---

## Executive summary

**Ship** the **Memories** subsystem in this monorepo: multimodal memories (images, audio, text, transcripts), **aligned** with the [Ohana Way prototype handoff](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md) for data shape, capture resilience, and HIPAA-oriented handling of memory PHI. **Do not** implement the full pilot (dashboard, inbox, AI Guide shell, practice admin) here—integrate via contracts.

**Success:** Authorized users capture and retrieve memories without **losing work** to flaky connectivity (client-owned queue/retry); media and transcripts are **access-controlled**; logs carry **no PHI content**; requirements stay traceable via **FR-** / **NFR-** IDs.

**Risks:** BAA/vendor lead time; offline capture engineering cost; unclear **system-of-record** split vs. Dashboard/other APIs.

---

## Background & scope boundary

**Ohana Way** (handoff) is the Guide-facing PWA and platform story—**Memory Capture** during visits, **Practice** multi-tenancy, **Client** vs **User**, `ClientAccess` RBAC, HIPAA, pilot MVP (handoff Section 12.1). **This repo** is the **memory vertical** ([README](../README.md)): web + API + shared contracts, eventually surfaced from a broader **Dashboard**.

**In this repo (near-term):** memory CRUD; list/detail; **MemoryMedia** (multiple) + **MemoryTranscript** (separate from blobs); signed uploads → server assigns `memory_id`; async transcription + UI states; idempotent save + **offline-tolerant** client retry (handoff Sections 2.5, 5.1); optional **suggest_prompt** + fallback (Section 6.2); phased tag suggestions, reactions/comments, `sharing_visibility`; audit events for memory PHI writes (Sections 4.4, 8.3).

**Elsewhere (cross-repo / pilot):** auth + MFA, G1/G2/G3, G6 inbox, G4 AI Guide **UI**, family invite/onboarding flows, Web Push orchestration, full RLS story if identity lives outside this service—**contracts** must still match handoff Sections 4, 7–8.

**Out of scope (near-term):** telephony/video, practice analytics dashboard, PDF memory book (handoff Section 12.2–12.3). **Non-goals:** native apps (PWA/browser first); SMS notifications; building full pilot **inside** this repo unless explicitly rescoped.

**Dependencies:** BAAs before prod PHI (Section 8.5); STT pick (Sections 3.4, 13.1); Dashboard/identity claims (`practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id`, roles).

---

## Problem & goals

**Problem:** Moments split across camera rolls, voice memos, and notes; **Ohana Way** adds risk of **losing** irreplaceable on-site captures without resilient upload behavior.

**Goals:** (1) Create/view/list memories with images, audio, text, and async transcripts + clear states. (2) Model matches handoff **Memory / MemoryMedia / MemoryTranscript** (Section 4.2). (3) Capture path resilient to connectivity (Section 5.1). (4) No PHI in app/analytics logs; audit when in HIPAA scope (Sections 8.3, 11.1). (5) Prompt suggestion <2s p95 with safe fallback (Section 6.2).

---

## Users

| Segment | Notes | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Guide** | Captures for client (Ohana Way) | Primary when this backs Guide flows |
| **Memory keeper / family** | Consumer / Dashboard framing | Primary when that mode ships first |
| **Client** | Often not a User in v1; access via `ClientAccess` | Subject |
| **Family** | Read / limited write per matrix (Section 7.2) | Phased |

Do not ship **user_id–only** authz that cannot grow into `ClientAccess`-shaped rules.

---

## Solution (one paragraph)

A **Memory** is metadata (e.g. name, room; optional era/valence per Section 4.3) plus **media** in object storage, **transcript** rows, optional **tags**, and later **reactions/comments** (C4). Flows: handoff **Memory Capture** (Section 5.1); async STT + optional tag pass (Sections 5.1, 6.3); detail playback; future **Dashboard** deep-link with same identity/tenant context.

---

## Key scenarios (compressed)

1. **Guide capture:** Photo → name + room → AI prompt (or fallback) → record → review → save → list; transcript and optional tags later; offline → queued retry.  
2. **Transcription:** pending → ready/failed without corrupting other fields; optional tag accept/reject.  
3. **Family:** Read allowed memories per visibility; create/delete per Section 7.2 matrix.  
4. **Dashboard (future):** Authenticated user sees only authorized library.

---

## Functional requirements

| ID | Requirement | P | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **FR-001** | Create memory (scoped to authorized client/tenant). | P0 | Tenant fields in TDD |
| **FR-002** | View detail: text + media + transcript when ready. | P0 | |
| **FR-003** | Update allowed fields per Section 7.2 matrix. | P0 | Per-role fields in TDD |
| **FR-004** | Delete where policy allows + confirm. | P1 | Family may not delete (Section 7.2) |
| **FR-005** | One or more images as **MemoryMedia**. | P0 | Section 4.2 |
| **FR-006** | Audio attach + playback; STT-friendly format (e.g. AAC/M4A). | P0 | Section 5.1 |
| **FR-007** | Text fields with limits; **name + room** required in Guide flow; consumer may relax room—PM call. | P0 | Section 5.1 |
| **FR-008** | Async transcription → **MemoryTranscript** (+ confidence if vendor). | P0 | Sections 4.2, 5.1, 12.1 |
| **FR-009** | Transcription UI: pending / ready / failed. | P0 | |
| **FR-010** | List with cursor pagination (~20), recency default. | P0 | Section 5.5 |
| **FR-011** | Reject bad media clearly; ~2000px client resize target in capture. | P0 | Section 5.1 |
| **FR-012** | Authz on every read/write: tenant + `ClientAccess` analog. | P0 | Sections 7.3–7.4; RLS vs app in TDD |
| **FR-013** | Idempotent create-after-upload (no duplicate memories on retry). | P0 | Section 5.1 |
| **FR-014** | Offline-tolerant save: retain artifacts, retry (e.g. backoff, up to 24h window per handoff). | P0 | Sections 2.5, 5.1, 12.1 |
| **FR-015** | `suggest_prompt`-style API: minimal context in, one question or fallback on failure/timeout; p95 <2s. | P1 | Section 6.2 |
| **FR-016** | Post-transcript tag suggestions; accept/reject → **MemoryTag**. | P2 | Sections 6.3, 12.2 |
| **FR-017** | Reactions + comments on detail per roles. | P1 | Section 12.1, C4 |
| **FR-018** | `sharing_visibility` (or equivalent) before broad family defaults. | P2 | Sections 4.3, 13.2 |
| **FR-019** | Audit PHI-bearing memory creates/updates/deletes. | P0 | Sections 4.4, 8.3 |

*P = P0 launch-critical / P1 important / P2 follow-on.*

---

## Stories & acceptance (summary)

- **CRUD / list:** Given Guide with client access, after successful capture save → memory appears in paginated list with correct metadata and media. Given Family with visibility, opening memory shows media + transcript, not practice-only artifacts (e.g. GuideNote—outside Memories scope).
- **Resilient save:** Given failed upload/API, client retries per TDD without duplicate rows (**FR-013**).
- **Transcription / tags:** Transcript states per **FR-009**; optional tag flow per **FR-016**.

---

## Non-functional requirements

| ID | Category | Requirement | P | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **NFR-001** | Security | HTTPS in prod (TLS 1.2+, prefer 1.3). | P0 | Section 8.2 |
| **NFR-002** | Privacy | Signed URLs / least privilege; no anonymous PHI media. | P0 | Sections 3.3, 8 |
| **NFR-003** | Reliability | Health + structured errors; SLOs in TDD. | P1 | Section 11 |
| **NFR-004** | A11y | WCAG 2.2 AA intent on primary flows. | P1 | |
| **NFR-005** | Performance | Prompt p95 <2s; async STT 30s–3m acceptable. | P1 | Sections 5.1, 6.2 |
| **NFR-006** | Observability | Request correlation; **metadata-only** logs—no transcript/audio/content. | P0 | Sections 5.1 PHI, 11.1 |
| **NFR-007** | Compliance | BAAs before prod for all PHI-touching vendors. | P0 | Section 8.5 |
| **NFR-008** | Compliance | Audit authn, authz denials, PHI reads/writes per policy. | P0 | Section 8.3; may be platform-wide |
| **NFR-009** | Security | AI injection defenses; human confirm for privileged actions. | P0 | Section 6.5 |
| **NFR-010** | Ops | Monitor upload/AI failure rates; alert thresholds per Section 11.3. | P1 | |

---

## Analytics, GTM, migration

- **Metrics (proposal):** Captures per active client, transcription success rate, upload retry rate, prompt latency, list p95 (Section 11.2). Events: generic/redacted payloads until PHI policy decided (Section 10.3).
- **Pilot launch (this service’s slice):** Resilient capture, storage, transcript, detail, observability hooks—subset of Section 12.1. Audience: handoff pilot scale (Section 1.3).
- **Migration:** Forward migrations; soft-delete preference (Sections 4.4, 9.6). Ops: backups/restore per Section 11.4—often platform-owned.

---

## Milestones, risks, open questions

**Milestones:** TDD v0.1 (maps FR/NFR to APIs, storage, authz source, offline queue); STT eval (~20 samples, Section 3.4); cross-product MVP pilot Section 12.1 (TBD timeline ~3–6 mo order-of-magnitude, Section 12.4).

**Risks:** BAA lead time → parallel legal + synthetic staging (Sections 8.5–8.6); offline capture cost → timeboxed spike (Section 12.4); repo boundary → explicit contracts in TDD; PHI in notifications/logs → generic notifications + redaction (Sections 10.3, 11.1).

**Open questions:** (1) Is this API **system of record** for Practice/Client/Memory vs. **BFF** to another service? (2) STT vendor. (3) Cloud path A/B/C vs current Fastify+Postgres. (4) Consumer mode: require **room** or not? (5) Family accounts vs link-only for pilot (Section 13.1). (6) PHI in notifications (Section 10.3). (7) Death-of-client retention (Sections 9.4, 13.1).

---

## Appendix

**Glossary:** **Ohana Way** — full product (handoff). **Memory Capture** — four-step flow (Section 5.1). **Practice / Client / ClientAccess** — tenant + access (Section 4). **Memory / MemoryMedia / MemoryTranscript** — persistence (Section 4.2).

**References:** [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md); [Notion](https://plump-cheddar-f44.notion.site/Prototype-Backend-Engineering-Handoff-349fc9f5d1f880968428eac8506f728a?pvs=73); [README](../README.md); [tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md).

---

## Handoff

Author **technical-design.md** (`.cursor/skills/technical-designer/`) with FR/NFR traceability and handoff section cites for capture, authz, logging, AI. Then **development-planner** after TDD sketch. Keep handoff revs in sync with **Related docs** here.

1. **Executive summary** — States the product must be a **very simple app** for **elders**: few steps, plain language, large controls, clear feedback; favor richness in the **memory**, not in **UI chrome**. **Success** now references **NFR-012**.

5. **Stories & acceptance** — New bullet: **Elder-friendly** completion of capture/playback without extra coaching, aligned with **NFR-012**.

6. **NFR-012 (P0)** — **Elder-friendly UX**: minimal choices per step, plain-language copy, large touch targets, one clear primary action where practical, no hidden gestures required for core tasks; secondary features must not clutter default paths.

7. **NFR-004** — Notes it **complements NFR-012** (readability, focus, labels).

8. **Handoff** — TDD should trace **NFR-012**.

<user_query>
Now I want to add the workflow for the Memories app with screenshots. This should be applicable to both the PRD, technical-design, and wireframes. What's the best way to do this? Do each screenshot as a new message and let you know when I'm done?
</user_query>

You do **not** need one screenshot per message. That works, but it is slower for you and fragments context. Prefer one of these:

## Recommended: one canonical doc + image files in the repo

1. **Put images in the repo** under something like `docs/assets/memories-workflow/` (or `docs/images/memories/`), with clear names, e.g. `01-list-memories.png`, `02-capture-photo.png`.
2. **Add one markdown doc** that is the **single source of truth** for the flow, e.g. `docs/memories-user-workflow.md`, with:
   - A **numbered list** of steps (aligned with PRD scenarios / FRs).
   - Each step: short title, 1–2 sentences, then `![…](assets/memories-workflow/01-….png)`.
3. **Point the other docs at it** instead of duplicating galleries:
   - **PRD:** a short “Workflow visuals” subsection: link to `docs/memories-user-workflow.md` + one line on how it maps to journeys/FR IDs.
   - **Technical design:** same link + a small table (step → screen → API / component)—screenshots stay out of the TDD body if you want it lean.
   - **Wireframes:** link the same doc as **reference screenshots**, or note “high-fidelity reference; wireframes in …” per your `designer-wireframe` / `docs/templates/design-wireframe-template.md` process.

That way screenshots live **once**, version with git, and PRD/TDD/wireframes stay aligned by **link + step IDs**.

## If you prefer chat instead of committing files first

Send **one message** with **all** screenshots attached (Cursor allows multiple images), and in the same message a numbered list: `1) … 2) …` matching each image order. If a step has two screens, say so. You only need “done” if you split across messages (e.g. “Part 2 of 2—done”).

## When “one screenshot per message” *is* useful

Only if images are huge, you’re iterating one screen at a time, or you want threaded discussion per step. Otherwise batching is better.

---

**Summary:** Best path is **repo folder + one workflow markdown** referenced from PRD, technical design, and wireframes—not three separate screenshot galleries, and not necessarily one image per message. When you have the images in the repo (or one batched chat), say the path or paste the list and I can wire the cross-links into `product-requirements.md` and suggest the same block for future `technical-design.md` / wireframe doc.

<user_query>
How do I add an image to a markdown file?
</user_query>

In Markdown you embed an image with **alt text** and a **path or URL** to the file:

```markdown
![Short description of the image for accessibility](path/to/image.png)
```

**Rules that usually matter in a repo:**

1. **Path is relative to the Markdown file**  
   From `docs/product-requirements.md` to something under `docs/assets/`:

   ```markdown
   ![Memory list screen](assets/workflow-screenshots/01-list.png)
   ```

   From `docs/memories-user-workflow.md` in the same `docs/` folder, that same path works.

2. **Spaces in folder or file names**  
   Prefer names **without spaces** (`01-list.png`). If you must use spaces, encode them in the URL:

   ```markdown
   ![List](assets/workflow-screenshots/my%20screen.png)
   ```

3. **Git**  
   Commit the image file; anyone who clones the repo will see it in GitHub/Cursor previews the same way.

4. **Remote images**  
   Full URL works the same:

   ```markdown
   ![Diagram](https://example.com/image.png)
   ```

**Optional:** link the image so a click opens a larger version:

```markdown
[![Memory list (thumbnail)](assets/workflow-screenshots/01-list.png)](assets/workflow-screenshots/01-list.png)
```

Your `docs/assets/workflow-screenshots` entry should be a **folder** containing `.png` / `.jpg` files; if Cursor shows it as a 2-line “file,” rename or fix that so it’s a real directory with image files inside.

# Memories — user workflow (hi-fi + wireframe reference)

## Document control

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Purpose** | Single place for **capture → save → list** flow: screenshots, flow diagram, and low-fi layout notes. |
| **Used by** | [product-requirements.md](product-requirements.md), future `technical-design.md`, design/wireframe work |
| **Screenshots** | `docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/*.png` (7 steps, committed to repo) |
| **Last updated** | 2026-04-22 |

---

## Flow overview (mermaid)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[1 Photograph] --> B[2 Name and Room]
  B --> C[3a Prompt]
  C --> D[3b Recording]
  D --> E[4 Review and Save]
  E --> F[Success]
  F --> G[Memories list]
```

**Context bar (all capture steps):** “Facilitating for [Client]” — Guide-led capture for an elder; aligns with handoff and PRD `ClientAccess` / facilitator model.

---

## Step-by-step (hi-fi screenshots)

Paths are relative to this file (`docs/`).

### 1 — Photograph (Step 1 of 4)

![Step 1: photograph the object](assets/workflow-screenshots/01-step-1-photograph.png)

- Header: back, title **Capture Memory**, close.
- Progress: **Step 1 of 4 · Photograph**, ~25%.
- Primary: camera / viewfinder area; optional **Choose from Library**.
- Primary CTA: **Continue**.

### 2 — Name and room (Step 2 of 4)

![Step 2: object name and room chips](assets/workflow-screenshots/02-step-2-name-room.png)

- Photo thumbnail + retake.
- **Object name** (required); **Room** chips (required in Guide flow per PRD **FR-007**).
- CTA: **Continue**.

### 3a — Story prompt (Step 3 of 4, before record)

![Step 3a: Ohana Guide suggested prompt](assets/workflow-screenshots/03-step-3a-prompt-record.png)

- Memory summary card (title + room).
- **Ohana Guide suggests** — warm question for facilitator to ask elder (maps to **FR-015**).
- Mic CTA + alternates: record video / type or transcribe.

### 3b — Recording (Step 3 of 4, active)

![Step 3b: listening / recording state](assets/workflow-screenshots/04-step-3b-recording.png)

- Stop control; status (e.g. “Listening to …”).
- Same prompt card for context.

### 4 — Review and save (Step 4 of 4)

![Step 4: review audio, optional tags](assets/workflow-screenshots/05-step-4-review-save.png)

- Progress 100%; playback + **Re-record**.
- Optional **Tags** (chips + add).
- CTA: **Save to [Client]'s Archive** — idempotent save / offline queue per **FR-013**, **FR-014**.

### 5 — Success

![Success: memory saved confirmation](assets/workflow-screenshots/06-success-saved.png)

- Confirmation + **View in Archive** / **Capture another memory**.

### 6 — Memories list (client context)

![Memories tab: list with FAB](assets/workflow-screenshots/07-memories-list.png)

- Client header + tabs; **Memories** active; cards (thumbnail, title, snippet, “Added …”).
- FAB **+** for new capture (**FR-010** pagination / counts as in TDD).

---

## Low-fi wireframe (ASCII) — same flow

Use for quick reviews when PNGs are not open. Boxes = major regions, not pixel-accurate.

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ < Capture Memory        X │  │ < Capture Memory        X │
│ [ Facilitating for … ]    │  │ [ Facilitating for … ]    │
│ Step 1/4 · Photo   25%    │  │ Step 2/4 · Name    50%    │
│ [====···············]     │  │ [========···········]     │
│                             │  │ [ thumb ] Photo · Retake │
│  Let's capture…            │  │ What is this?            │
│  [   camera / preview   ]  │  │ [ Object name________ ]  │
│  Or choose from Library     │  │ ROOM: [Living][Bed]…     │
│ [      Continue         ] │  │ [      Continue         ] │
└─────────────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 3/4 · Story    75%     │  │ Step 3/4 · recording        │
│ [ summary card ]            │  │ [ summary ] [ prompt box ] │
│ ┌ Ohana Guide suggests ─┐  │  │        ( STOP )           │
│ │  “Ask …”              │  │  │   Listening…            │ │
│ └────────────────────────┘  │  │  video | type instead   │ │
│        ( MIC )              │  └─────────────────────────────┘
│  Tap when ready…            │
└─────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 4/4 · Review   100%    │  │ ✓ Memory saved            │
│ [ summary ]                 │  │ …in archive…              │
│ Play · waveform · Re-record │  │ [ View in Archive      ]  │
│ TAGS optional · +Add        │  │ Capture another memory    │
│ [ Save to … Archive      ]  │  └─────────────────────────────┘
└─────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ <  [EK] Eleanor Kim  …      │
│ Overview | Journey | Mem* │
│ RECENTLY ADDED    42 mem   │
│ ┌─────────────────────────┐│
│ │ [thumb] Title… snippet  ││
│ └─────────────────────────┘│
│                        ( + )│
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## PRD / TDD trace (quick)

| UI step | PRD (examples) | TDD will specify |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Photo / library | **FR-005**, **FR-011** | Upload, MIME/size, client resize |
| Name / room | **FR-007** | Validation, i18n limits |
| Prompt | **FR-015** | API contract, fallback copy, latency **NFR-005** |
| Record / playback | **FR-006**, **FR-008**, **FR-009** | MediaRecorder, storage, STT job |
| Review / tags | **FR-016**, **FR-017** (phased) | Tag model, permissions |
| Save | **FR-013**, **FR-014**, **FR-019** | Idempotency, queue, audit |
| List | **FR-010**, **FR-012** | Pagination, authz |
| Simplicity | **NFR-012** | Copy deck, touch targets, max actions per screen |

---

## Next steps for you

1. **Commit** `docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/*.png` and this file with git.  
2. **Technical design:** add a section that links here and maps each step to routes, API calls, and state machines.  
3. **Wireframes:** treat this doc as **reference hi-fi**; use ASCII above or designer-wireframe skill for empty/loading/error states not shown in screenshots.

You do **not** need to re-save screenshots elsewhere if they live under `docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/` as committed files (already copied from Cursor’s asset cache into the repo).

Here’s a direct answer to both questions, plus what I already did in the repo.

## Do you need to save each screenshot again?

**No.** Those seven images were copied from Cursor’s asset cache into your **Memories** repo with **stable filenames**:

`docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/01-step-1-photograph.png` … `07-memories-list.png`

Commit them with git; that’s your canonical set. You only replace files in that folder when the UI changes (names stay the same so links don’t break). There’s a short [docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/README.md](docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/README.md) explaining that.

## Can a wireframe be created from them?

**Yes, in two layers (both useful):**

1. **Hi-fi “wireframe”** = the **screenshots themselves** (best for visual/design fidelity). No need to redraw the same screens unless you want a stripped-down variant.
2. **Lo-fi wireframe** = quick **ASCII + mermaid** (and later empty/loading/error states) so engineers and PM can scan flows without opening every PNG. I added that in **[docs/memories-user-workflow.md](docs/memories-user-workflow.md)** along with embedded screenshots and a **PRD/TDD trace** table.

So: **screenshots for truth**, **ASCII/mermaid for speed**, optional Figma/low-fi later for states not in the shots.

## What’s the next step?

1. **`git add` / commit** `docs/memories-user-workflow.md`, `docs/assets/workflow-screenshots/*`, and the PRD/tech-stack updates (**PRD is now v0.6** and links the workflow doc).
2. **`docs/technical-design.md`**: one section “UI workflow” → link [memories-user-workflow.md](docs/memories-user-workflow.md) and a step→route/API table (when you author TDD).
3. **Wireframes / design**: use the same doc as reference; add **states not shown** (empty list, upload failure, transcription failed, offline banner) with the designer-wireframe skill or your template.

# Memories — technical design

## Document control

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Author** | Ken Levy |
| **Engineering owner** | TBD |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Version** | 0.1 |
| **Last updated** | 2026-04-22 |
| **Template used** | `docs/templates/technical-design-template.md` (structure); content scoped to Memories |
| **Related PRD** | [product-requirements.md](product-requirements.md) v0.6 |
| **Related docs** | [memories-user-workflow.md](memories-user-workflow.md); [design-wireframe.md](design-wireframe.md); [tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md); [Prototype Backend Engineering Handoff.md](Prototype%20Backend%20Engineering%20Handoff.md) |

---

## 1. Summary

- **Objective:** Implement the **Memories** vertical (web + API + `@memories/shared`) for multimodal memories—capture, storage, list/detail, async transcription, facilitator context, and pilot-aligned resilience—per PRD **FR-001**–**FR-019** and **NFR-001**–**NFR-012**.
- **Non-goals (technical):** Full Ohana Way shell (G1/G6/G4 UI), practice billing, messaging, AI Guide chat backend—unless explicitly merged; native apps.
- **Current codebase:** `apps/api` exposes **`GET /health`** only; `apps/web` is a splash screen. Routes and tables below are **targets** for implementation, not existing endpoints.

---

## 2. Context

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph clients [Browser PWA]
    Web[apps/web - React/Vite]
  end
  subgraph backend [apps/api]
    API[Fastify HTTP]
  end
  subgraph data [TBD]
    DB[(PostgreSQL)]
    Obj[Object storage - S3 compatible]
  end
  subgraph vendors [PHI - BAA required]
    STT[Speech-to-text]
    AI[LLM - prompt / tags]
  end
  Web --> API
  API --> DB
  API --> Obj
  API --> STT
  API --> AI
```

**Identity:** JWT/session claims (`practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id`, roles) from platform or this service—**open** (PRD open question #1). All memory APIs must enforce **tenant + client access** (**FR-012**).

---

## 3. UI workflow (screen → route → API)

**Hi-fi reference:** step screenshots, mermaid, ASCII, and PRD trace table in **[memories-user-workflow.md](memories-user-workflow.md)**.

**Wireframe reference (empty/error/offline):** **[design-wireframe.md](design-wireframe.md)**.

### 3.1 Step table (implementation target)

| Step | Screen ID (wireframe) | User-visible step | Proposed web route (TBD) | Proposed API / integration (TBD) | PRD |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | **ML1** | Client **Memories** tab (list + FAB) | `GET /clients/:clientId/memories` view or `/c/:clientId/memories` | `GET /api/clients/:clientId/memories?cursor=` (cursor pagination **FR-010**) | **FR-010**, **FR-012** |
| 1 | **MC1** | Photograph | `/clients/:clientId/capture` step `photo` | `POST /api/uploads/images/sign` → direct PUT to object storage; local IndexedDB draft id | **FR-005**, **FR-011**, **FR-014** |
| 2 | **MC2** | Name & room | same route, step `meta` | PATCH draft or hold in client until **POST /api/memories** finalize | **FR-007** |
| 3a | **MC3** | Story prompt (pre-record) | step `prompt` | `POST /api/memories/suggest_prompt` (handoff Section 6.2); fallback copy on error | **FR-015**, **NFR-005**, **NFR-009** |
| 3b | **MC4** | Recording | step `record` | Client `MediaRecorder`; audio blob → `POST /api/uploads/audio/sign` → PUT; optional websocket **not** MVP | **FR-006**, **FR-014** |
| 4 | **MC5** | Review & save | step `review` | `POST /api/memories` idempotent (**FR-013**) body: client_id, keys, metadata, tag selections; server creates `memory_id`, enqueues STT | **FR-008**, **FR-009**, **FR-016**, **FR-019** |
| 5 | **MC6** | Success | step `done` | Same session; optional `GET /api/memories/:id` prefetch | **FR-002** |
| 6 | **ML1** | Return to list | navigate to list route | `GET` list; reflect new card | **FR-010** |

**Global chrome (all MC\*):** facilitator strip (“Facilitating for …”) = resolved from auth context + `clientId`; no PII in analytics payloads (**NFR-006**, handoff Section 10.3).

**Route naming:** Final paths may use TanStack Router / React Router prefix `/(app)/...`; table uses logical segments. **Single-page stepper** vs separate routes is an implementation choice—document in §9 when decided.

---

## 4. Requirements traceability (Memories)

| PRD ID | Design coverage |
| --- | --- |
| **FR-001**–**FR-004** | §3.1 create/view/update/delete; §6 authz middleware on memory routes |
| **FR-005**–**FR-007** | §3.1 upload + metadata; §5 `Memory` + `MemoryMedia` + text fields |
| **FR-008**–**FR-009** | §5 transcript job; §7 worker callback; client poll or SSE **TBD** |
| **FR-010** | §3.1 list cursor; §5 indexes |
| **FR-011** | §7 validation + client resize policy |
| **FR-012** | §2 identity; §6 authorization; RLS policies **TBD** if Postgres RLS |
| **FR-013**–**FR-014** | §7 idempotency keys; §8 offline queue contract (service worker + IndexedDB) |
| **FR-015** | §3.1 suggest_prompt; §7 Anthropic integration, redaction |
| **FR-016**–**FR-018** | §5 tags + visibility enum |
| **FR-019** | §7 audit sink append-only |
| **NFR-001**–**NFR-010** | §7 infra, logging, observability, alerts |
| **NFR-011** | [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md) coverage targets; CI gates |
| **NFR-012** | [design-wireframe.md](design-wireframe.md) density + component guidelines for eng |

---

## 5. Data model (sketch)

Align with handoff Section 4.2 (entity names may vary in migration naming):

- **`practices`**, **`users`**, **`clients`**, **`client_access`** — tenant and RBAC (if this service is system of record).
- **`memories`**: `id`, `client_id`, `practice_id`, title/name, room, optional body, `sharing_visibility`, timestamps, soft-delete.
- **`memory_media`**: `memory_id`, `type` (image|audio|…), `storage_key`, `sort_order`, mime, byte_size.
- **`memory_transcripts`**: `memory_id`, `text`, `status` (pending|ready|failed), `confidence`, vendor refs.
- **`memory_tags`**, **`memory_reactions`**, **`memory_comments`** — phased per **FR-016**, **FR-017**.

**Transcript job:** queue table or external worker; STT vendor per **NFR-007** / PRD open questions.

---

## 6. Components (repos)

| Component | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| **`apps/web`** | Capture stepper, list, facilitator chrome, offline queue, signed upload client (**FR-014**, **NFR-012**) |
| **`apps/api`** | Authz, signed URLs, memory CRUD, idempotent create, enqueue STT, suggest_prompt proxy |
| **`packages/shared`** | Zod contracts for API + shared constants |

---

## 7. Security, privacy, logging

- **TLS** end-to-end (**NFR-001**); object access via **short-lived signed URLs** (**NFR-002**).
- **Logs:** structured JSON; **metadata only**—no transcript text, names in URLs, or base64 media (**NFR-006**).
- **AI calls:** minimum necessary fields; delimiter wrapping for user content; zero-retention endpoint per vendor contract (**NFR-009**).
- **Audit:** memory PHI writes to append-only store (**FR-019**, **NFR-008**).

---

## 8. Offline capture (engineering notes)

Per handoff Sections 2.5 and 5.1:

- Client maintains **working memory** in **IndexedDB** (draft id, photo blob ref, audio blob ref, metadata) until **POST /api/memories** succeeds.
- **Retries:** exponential backoff, up to **24h** window (product); **Background Sync** where supported + foreground retry when app opens.
- **Idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key` header (or client-generated UUID in body) on create to satisfy **FR-013**.

---

## 9. Open decisions (technical)

| Topic | Options | Note |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Router | TanStack Router vs React Router | [tech-stack.md](tech-stack.md) open until locked |
| Stepper routing | Query `?step=` vs nested routes vs state-only | UX deep-link preference |
| Realtime | Poll transcript status vs SSE | PRD allows async |
| RLS | Postgres RLS vs app-only checks | Tied to PRD open question #1 |

---

## 10. Rollout

- Feature flags for STT and AI prompt in non-prod.
- Synthetic PHI **only** outside production until BAAs signed (**NFR-007**).

---

| Version | Date | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0.1 | 2026-04-22 | Initial TDD: UI workflow map, traceability, data sketch, offline/security notes |

## Document control

| Field | Value |

## Summary

This doc adds **screens and states not covered** by the committed hi-fi screenshots: **empty memories list**, **upload failure**, **transcription failed**, and **offline / saving** banner behavior. The **happy-path capture flow** (MC1–MC6 + list ML1) is illustrated in **PNG form** in [memories-user-workflow.md](memories-user-workflow.md)—do not duplicate those layouts here; reference that file for visual design.

**Principles:** **Mobile-first**, **NFR-012** (elder-friendly: few choices, plain language, large targets), trace screens to **FR** where noted.

---

## Hi-fi reference (do not redraw)

| Wireframe ID | Step | Hi-fi |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **MC1** | Photograph | [memories-user-workflow.md §1](memories-user-workflow.md) |
| **MC2** | Name & room | §2 |
| **MC3** | Prompt | §3a |
| **MC4** | Recording | §3b |
| **MC5** | Review & save | §4 |
| **MC6** | Success | §5 |
| **ML1** | Memories list | §6 |

---

## Screen inventory (supplementary)

| ID | Surface | Purpose | Primary actor |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **ML0** | Memories list **empty** | First visit or cleared list; invite capture | Guide / family |
| **ML-E1** | Upload / save **error** | Photo or audio failed to reach server; retry | Guide |
| **MC-E2** | Transcript **failed** | STT failed; show message + retry or save without transcript | Guide |
| **MC-G1** | **Offline / queued** banner | Work saved locally; “Will send when online” | Guide |
| **MC-G2** | **Saving** spinner | In-flight idempotent save after connectivity returns | Guide |

---

## Flow (happy + error branches)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  ML1[ML1 Memories list] --> MC1[MC1 Photo]
  MC1 --> MC2[MC2 Name room]
  MC2 --> MC3[MC3 Prompt]
  MC3 --> MC4[MC4 Record]
  MC4 --> MC5[MC5 Review save]
  MC5 -->|success| MC6[MC6 Saved]
  MC5 -->|upload error| ML_E1[ML-E1 Upload error]
  ML_E1 --> MC5
  MC5 -->|offline| MC_G1[MC-G1 Queued banner]
  MC_G1 --> MC5
  MC6 --> ML1
  ML1 -->|zero items| ML0[ML0 Empty list]
  ML1 -->|detail later| MC_E2[MC-E2 Transcript failed]
  MC_E2 --> ML1
```

---

## Low-fi layouts (supplementary states only)

### ML0 — Empty memories list [FR-010]

```
+------------------------------------------+
| <   [EK] Eleanor Kim     [In Transition] |
| Overview | Journey | Memories* | Docs  |
+------------------------------------------+
|  NO MEMORIES YET                         |
|  One short reassuring line of copy.      |
|                                          |
|  [    Capture first memory (large)   ] |
|                                          |
|  (optional: small “What is Memories?”)   |
+------------------------------------------+
```

- **Primary CTA:** one button → opens **MC1** capture flow.
- **Secondary:** none on first version unless PM adds help link.

---

### ML-E1 — Upload / save failed [FR-011, FR-014]

Shown as **modal** or **inline banner** on top of MC5 (review) or after failed finalize.

```
+------------------------------------------+
|  ! Couldn't save yet                     |
|  Check your connection and try again.    |
|  [ Retry save ]     [ Keep editing ]     |
+------------------------------------------+
```

- **Retry** reuses same idempotency key (**FR-013**).
- No technical jargon in copy (**NFR-012**).

---

### MC-E2 — Transcription failed [FR-009]

On **memory detail** or inline on card after list refresh.

```
+------------------------------------------+
|  Mom's Wedding Ring                      |
|  [ photo ]  [ play audio ]               |
|                                          |
|  Transcript unavailable                  |
|  We couldn't turn the recording into     |
|  text. You can retry or continue without.|
|  [ Retry transcript ]  [ Dismiss ]       |
+------------------------------------------+
```

- **Retry** kicks job again; **Dismiss** leaves memory valid without transcript.

---

### MC-G1 — Offline / queued [FR-014]

**Banner** below header on any capture step after local save.

```
+------------------------------------------+
|  ~ This memory is saved on this device   |
|    and will upload when you're online.   |
|    [ OK ]                                |
+------------------------------------------+
```

- Non-blocking where possible; **OK** dismisses for session (banner may return next visit if still queued).

---

### MC-G2 — Saving in progress

Thin **inline** status above primary CTA on MC5.

```
|  Saving… please keep this screen open.   |
|  [=========>·······]                     |
```

---

## Global patterns

| Topic | Guidance |
| --- | --- |
| **Touch** | Primary actions full-width where possible; min target **44px** equivalent (**NFR-012**). |
| **Errors** | One sentence **what** + one **what to do**; never raw status codes. |
| **Loading** | Prefer skeleton on list; spinner on save only when blocking. |
| **Focus** | Dialogs trap focus; Escape returns to predictable step (per frontend-ui skill at build time). |

---

## Traceability

| Screen ID | FR / NFR |
| --- | --- |
| ML0 | **FR-010** (empty state UX) |
| ML-E1 | **FR-011**, **FR-013**, **FR-014** |
| MC-E2 | **FR-008**, **FR-009** |
| MC-G1, MC-G2 | **FR-014**, **NFR-003** |
| All MC\*, ML\* | **NFR-012**, **NFR-004** |

---

## Open questions

1. **Empty list:** Is “Capture first memory” always shown, or hidden when user is read-only family?
2. **Upload error:** Modal vs sticky banner—preference from design review?
3. **Transcript failed:** Only on detail view, or also toast from list?

content.text:
Yes — using **filename editions** (`*-v1.md`, later `*-v2.md`) for these three “living” docs is a solid approach: it keeps **history obvious in git**, avoids silent overwrites, and matches how you already think about **in-doc** `Version` / revision tables.

Here is what was done:

### Renamed (canonical paths now)

- `docs/product-requirements-v1.md` — **Version** set to **1.0**, new **Edition** row explains the `*-v1` file rule; fixed the broken handoff link (“prototype handoff”); **Revision (file)** table added; all links point at `technical-design-v1` / `design-wireframe-v1`.
- `docs/technical-design-v1.md` — **Version 1.0**, **Edition** row, PRD link to `product-requirements-v1`, wireframe links updated, revision row for file v1.
- `docs/design-wireframe-v1.md` — **Version 1.0**, **Edition** row, PRD/TDD links to `*-v1`, revision row.

Removed the stray duplicate `docs/product-requirements.md` (the non-`-v1` copy had reappeared).

### Cross-references updated

`memories-user-workflow.md`, `tech-stack.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/rules/docs-governance.mdc` (also lists `design-wireframe-v1` for UI changes), `developer-manager`, `developer-backend`, `developer-database`, `developer-senior`, `developer-frontend-ui`, and `email-deliverability.md`.

### Convention (matches what you described)

- **Small edits:** same `*-v1.md` file + a line in the **Revision** table (or bump **Version** as you prefer).
- **Large / incompatible change:** new file **`product-requirements-v2.md`** (etc.) and update pointers in `tech-stack.md`, `AGENTS.md`, governance, and skills again.

`docs/templates/*` still mention the **template** multi-list filenames on purpose; they are not the Memories trilogy.

If you want **`memories-user-workflow.md`** to follow the same pattern (`memories-user-workflow-v1.md`), say so and we can align it.
